name: Red Chuchu Jelly
effect: Fiery jelly that bursts into flame when struck or thrown (a free fire source). Upgrades the cold-resisting Snowquill set (Headdress, Tunic, Trousers) at Great Fairies; cooks into a warming elixir.
where: Dropped by Fire Chuchu (hot regions like Eldin), or made by hitting plain Chuchu Jelly with fire.

name: Red Lizalfos Tail
effect: The fiery tail of a Fire-Breath Lizalfos. Upgrades the cold-resisting Snowquill set (Headdress, Tunic, Trousers) at Great Fairies, and cooks into a warming (cold-resist) elixir.
where: Dropped by Fire-Breath Lizalfos, common in the hot Eldin region.

name: Rock Salt
effect: A pinkish mineral, not a true gem. It has no stat effect of its own but, when added to a dish or elixir, it can be used to season many salt-grilled recipes (and on its own makes Dubious Food). A near-worthless sell item, so keep it for cooking.
where: Smash Ore Deposits (which sometimes yield it) or buy it from general/item shops.

name: Ruby
effect: A red gem. Used to buy and upgrade the Ruby Circlet, which grants Cold Resistance (keeps you warm in cold regions), at Starlight Memories in Gerudo Town. The Ruby Circlet is one of the three accessories you can choose as the reward for the 'Tools of the Trade' quest. Otherwise a high-value sell item. (Not used to craft any weapon in BotW.)
where: Smash Ore Deposits and Rare Ore Deposits, open chests, or defeat Stone Taluses and Stone Pebblits.

name: Sapphire
effect: A blue gem. Used to buy and upgrade the Sapphire Circlet, which grants Heat Resistance (keeps you cool in hot regions like the Gerudo Desert), at Starlight Memories in Gerudo Town. The Sapphire Circlet is one of the three accessories you can choose as the reward for the 'Tools of the Trade' quest. Otherwise a high-value sell item. (Dayfah in the Gerudo desert oasis buys Sapphires for 650 rupees.)
where: Smash Ore Deposits and Rare Ore Deposits, open chests, or defeat Stone Taluses and Stone Pebblits.

name: Shard of Dinraal's Fang
effect: A fang shard from the fire dragon Dinraal — a rare armor-upgrade material at Great Fairies and a high-value sell. Cooks into a potent 30-minute fire/attack dish.
where: Shoot Dinraal's mouth (the red fire dragon) as it flies over the Eldin region.

name: Shard of Dinraal's Horn
effect: The rarest Dinraal part. In any dish/elixir it extends the effect to a full 30 minutes; it also tops out the Barbarian Helm, Champion's Tunic, Trousers of the Wild, and Fierce Deity's Mask at Great Fairies. One of the most valuable monster sells in the game.
where: Shoot Dinraal's horn (the red fire dragon) as it flies over the Eldin region.

name: Shard of Farosh's Fang
effect: A fang shard from the lightning dragon Farosh — a rare armor-upgrade material at Great Fairies and a high-value sell. Cooks into a potent 30-minute shock/attack dish.
where: Shoot Farosh's mouth (the yellow-green lightning dragon) near Lake Hylia / Lanayru.

name: Shard of Farosh's Horn
effect: A horn shard from the lightning dragon Farosh. Extends a cooked dish or elixir's effect to 30 minutes and serves as a top-tier armor-upgrade material at Great Fairies. One of the most valuable monster sells in the game.
where: Shoot Farosh's horn (the yellow-green lightning dragon) near Lake Hylia / Lanayru.

name: Shard of Naydra's Fang
effect: A fang shard from the ice dragon Naydra — a rare armor-upgrade material at Great Fairies and a high-value sell. Cooks into a potent 30-minute cold/attack dish.
where: Shoot Naydra's mouth (the blue ice dragon) over the Lanayru region.

name: Shard of Naydra's Horn
effect: A horn shard from the ice dragon Naydra. Extends a cooked dish or elixir's effect to 30 minutes and is a top-tier armor-upgrade material at Great Fairies. Among the most valuable monster sells in the game.
where: Shoot Naydra's horn (the blue ice dragon) over the Lanayru region.

name: Spirit Orb
effect: A sacred orb given for clearing a Shrine. Bring four to any Goddess Statue to trade for one Heart Container (more max health) OR one Stamina Vessel (more stamina). Cannot be sold.
where: Earned from the Sheikah Monk at the end of each of the 120 Shrines. Cash four in at a Goddess Statue (the first is in the Temple of Time on the Great Plateau; others sit in towns and near Great Fairy areas).

name: Star Fragment
effect: A rare, glittering material from a fallen star. Its main use is end-game armor upgrades: a Star Fragment is needed for the ★4 (final) tier of many sets at the Great Fairy Fountains, including the Ancient, Wild (Hero), and amiibo sets, the Diamond/Ruby/Sapphire Circlets and Topaz Earrings, and the Divine (Vah) Helms. Cannot be bought. Also a guaranteed critical-cook ingredient and a high-value sell item.
where: At night, watch for a shooting star and note where its light lands, then travel there and grab the glowing fragment before it fades. They fall most often after dark (roughly 9pm-midnight); standing on a high point makes them easier to spot.

name: Topaz
effect: A yellow gem. Used to buy and upgrade the Topaz Earrings, which grant Shock (electricity) Resistance, at Starlight Memories in Gerudo Town. The Topaz Earrings are also one of the three accessories you can choose as the reward for the 'Tools of the Trade' quest. Otherwise a valuable sell item. (Not used to craft any weapon in BotW.)
where: Smash Ore Deposits (and the rarer Rare Ore Deposits), open chests, or defeat Stone Taluses and Stone Pebblits.

name: White Chuchu Jelly
effect: Freezing jelly that bursts into ice/cold when struck or thrown. Upgrades the heat-resisting Desert Voe set (Headband, Spaulder, Trousers) at Great Fairies; cooks into a cooling elixir.
where: Dropped by Ice Chuchu (cold regions), or made by freezing plain Chuchu Jelly (e.g. drop a blue Chuchu in cold water).

name: Wood
effect: A plain bundle of wood. Drop it with a piece of Flint and strike the flint with a metal weapon to start a campfire (pass time / cook). Also a quest/building material (Tarrey Town's 'From the Ground Up') and a few NPCs ask for it. Low sell value.
where: Chop trees anywhere (axe, fire, or a Remote Bomb to fell several) and pick up the logs they leave; plentiful around Rito Stable, Satori Mountain, and Kakariko Village.

name: Yellow Chuchu Jelly
effect: Electrified jelly that discharges a shock when struck or thrown. Upgrades the shock-resisting Rubber set (Helm, Armor, Tights) at Great Fairies; cooks into a shock-resist elixir.
where: Dropped by Electric Chuchu, or made by hitting plain Chuchu Jelly with electricity.

name: Yellow Lizalfos Tail
effect: The electrified tail of an Electric Lizalfos. Upgrades the shock-resisting Rubber set (Helm, Armor, Tights) at Great Fairies, and cooks into a shock-resist elixir.
where: Dropped by Electric Lizalfos, found in wet/desert areas like the Gerudo region.

name: Armored Carp
effect: A hardy carp; cooked it grants a temporary defense-up (Tough).
where: Rivers and lakes - Lanayru, Hyrule Field, Akkala fresh water.

name: Armored Porgy
effect: A saltwater fish; cooked it grants a temporary defense-up (Tough).
where: Coastal salt water - Necluda Sea, Lanayru Sea, Faron coastlines.

name: Bladed Rhino Beetle
effect: A horned beetle; cooked with a monster part it makes a Mighty Elixir for a temporary attack-up boost.
where: On trees in West Necluda and East Necluda.

name: Blue-Winged Heron
effect: A wading heron; shooting it down yields Raw Bird Drumstick / Raw Bird Thigh (Cook-tab food).
where: Wetlands and riverbanks - Hyrule Ridge and Faron Grasslands.

name: Blupee
effect: A shy, glowing blue rabbit-like spirit. Cannot be tamed or eaten - shoot it with arrows before it teleports away and it drops rupees (more hits = more rupees).
where: Appears at night in forests, hills, and especially on Satori Mountain. Hit it fast before it flees.

name: Bright-Chested Duck
effect: A wild duck; shooting it down yields Raw Bird Drumstick / Raw Bird Thigh (Cook-tab food).
where: Near water - Hyrule Ridge and Hyrule Field.

name: Bright-Eyed Crab
effect: A freshwater crab; cooked it restores stamina (Energizing).
where: Freshwater shores - Lanayru Great Spring, Eldin Canyon, around Ne'ez Yohma Shrine in Zora's Domain.

name: Bushy-Tailed Squirrel
effect: A small forest squirrel; harmless critter with no huntable material drop.
where: Wooded areas - Hyrule Field and Hyrule Ridge.

name: Chillfin Trout
effect: An icy trout; cooked it grants cold resistance (Chilly). Attracted by Chillshrooms.
where: Cold water - Hebra Mountains, Tabantha Frontier, Mount Lanayru.

name: Cold Darner
effect: A dragonfly that thrives in heat; cooked with a monster part it makes a Chilly Elixir for heat resistance.
where: Cold zones - Tabantha Frontier and Hyrule Ridge. Also sold by Beedle.

name: Cold-Footed Wolf
effect: A snow wolf that hunts in packs and attacks Link; killing it yields Raw Prime Meat or Raw Gourmet Meat (Cook-tab food).
where: Hebra Mountains and Gerudo Highlands.

name: Common Sparrow
effect: A small songbird; shooting it down yields a Chickaloo Tree Nut (a Cook-tab food). Rare color variants (Red/Blue/Golden/Rainbow/Sand Sparrow) are the same compendium creature.
where: Fields and trees - Hyrule Field and Hyrule Ridge.

name: Cucco
effect: A village chicken. Don't attack it - hitting it repeatedly summons a swarm of vengeful Cuccos. Not a material; produces Bird Eggs in pens.
where: Villages and farms - Kakariko, Hateno, and stables across Necluda.

name: Dog
effect: A friendly domesticated dog (Hylian Retriever). Not a material - feed it 3 pieces of meat (or fruit) until happy and it leads you to a nearby buried treasure chest (dig/Magnesis it up).
where: Hangs around stables and villages across Hyrule (e.g. Hateno, Tarrey Town, various stables).

name: Eldin Ostrich
effect: A large flightless bird of the volcano region; shooting it down yields Raw Bird Drumstick / Raw Whole Bird (Cook-tab food).
where: Eldin Canyon and the Eldin Mountains.

name: Electric Darner
effect: A storm dragonfly; cooked with a monster part it makes an Electro Elixir for shock resistance.
where: Appears in rain/thunderstorms - Hyrule Ridge, Gerudo Desert, near Tabantha Bridge Stable.

name: Energetic Rhino Beetle
effect: A prized beetle; cooked with a monster part it makes an Energizing Elixir that restores stamina.
where: On trees/pillars - Akkala Highlands (South Akkala Stable), West Necluda, Kakariko Village.

name: Fairy
effect: A tiny healing sprite. Carried in your pouch it auto-revives Link with 5 hearts when he falls (or heal manually); also cooks into restorative dishes.
where: Sneak-grab in tall grass and around Great Fairy Fountains/the Great Deku Tree, mostly at night. Only spawns while you hold 2 or fewer.

name: Fireproof Lizard
effect: A flame-resistant lizard; cooked with a monster part it makes a Fireproof Elixir for fire protection.
where: On rocks in the heat - Eldin Canyon and Death Mountain. Sneak to grab.

name: Giant Horse
effect: A huge, immensely strong stallion (the 'Lord of the Mountain'-scale beast resembling Ganon's horse): 5-star strength, 2-star speed, and NO stamina at all - a heavy, powerful but slow mount once tamed and registered.
where: Taobab Grassland in the Faron region (south of the Great Plateau). Needs 2+ full stamina wheels to soothe.

name: Grassland Fox
effect: A small wild fox; hunting it yields Raw Meat (a Cook-tab food). Very alert - needs stealth.
where: Hyrule Field and Lanayru Great Spring.

name: Great-Horned Rhinoceros
effect: A massive horned rhino; hunting it yields Raw Prime Meat or Raw Gourmet Meat (Cook-tab food).
where: Hebra Mountains and Gerudo Highlands.

name: Grizzlemaw Bear
effect: A large mountain bear; killing it yields Raw Prime Meat or Raw Gourmet Meat (Cook-tab food). Dangerous up close.
where: Hebra Mountains and Gerudo Highlands.

name: Hateno Cow
effect: A domesticated cow; livestock with no notable huntable drop.
where: Pastures of East Necluda (Hateno) and Faron Grasslands.

name: Hearty Bass
effect: A large bass; cooked it grants a Hearty effect (full heal + temporary bonus hearts).
where: Lakes and ponds - common around Hateno/East Necluda and other fresh water.

name: Hearty Blueshell Snail
effect: A beach snail; cooked it grants a Hearty effect (full heal + bonus hearts).
where: Sandy beaches in numbers - Lanayru Sea and Necluda Sea coasts.

name: Hearty Lizard
effect: A plump lizard; cooked with a monster part it makes a Hearty Elixir that fully heals and adds temporary bonus hearts.
where: Rocky/desert ground - Gerudo Desert and the Necluda coast. Uncommon.

name: Hearty Salmon
effect: A big salmon; cooked it grants a Hearty effect (full heal + bonus hearts) - a top early healing fish.
where: Cold rivers/lakes - Tabantha, Hebra, and Zora's Domain area waters.

name: Highland Sheep
effect: A domesticated sheep; livestock with no notable huntable drop.
where: East Necluda and Akkala Highlands farms.

name: Hightail Lizard
effect: A fast-darting lizard; cooked with a monster part it makes a Hasty Elixir for movement speed.
where: On the ground/rocks - West Necluda and East Necluda. Sneak to grab.

name: Honeyvore Bear
effect: A brown bear; killing it yields Raw Prime Meat, or Raw Gourmet Meat from larger ones (Cook-tab food). It can also be tamed and ridden (but not registered).
where: Akkala Highlands and Tabantha Frontier forests.

name: Horse
effect: The standard wild horse and your main mount. Sneak up, mount, soothe it to tame, then register it at any stable.
where: Roaming grasslands - Hyrule Field, Faron Grasslands, Lanayru. Approach crouched from behind.

name: Hot-Footed Frog
effect: A quick little frog; cooked with a monster part it makes a Hasty Elixir that boosts movement speed.
where: Near water/wet grass - Lanayru Great Spring and Hyrule Ridge. Sneak to grab.

name: Hyrule Bass
effect: The most common fish; cooked it just restores hearts (a neutral cooking base). Caught by hand, with a shock/ice attack, or bomb.
where: Calm fresh water almost everywhere - ponds, rivers, lakes across Hyrule.

name: Ironshell Crab
effect: A hard-shelled crab; cooked it grants a temporary defense-up (Tough).
where: Coastlines - Puffer Beach in the Faron Sea and beaches around Necluda.

name: Islander Hawk
effect: A bird of prey; shooting it down yields Raw Bird Drumstick (a Cook-tab food).
where: Open skies over Hyrule Field and Hyrule Ridge.

name: Lord of the Mountain
effect: Satori - a rare, glowing blue-green spirit mount with a horse body and twin Blupee faces. Top mount stats (max speed/strength, infinite stamina), but it vanishes once you dismount and cannot be registered.
where: Atop Satori Mountain (south Hyrule Ridge). Spawns on a night the peak glows green, surrounded by Blupees.

name: Maraudo Wolf
effect: A pack-hunting wolf that will attack you; killing it yields Raw Prime Meat or Raw Gourmet Meat (Cook-tab food).
where: Tabantha Frontier and Great Hyrule Forest.

name: Mighty Carp
effect: A carp; cooked it grants a temporary attack-up (Mighty).
where: Rivers and lakes - common in Lanayru and Akkala fresh water.

name: Mighty Porgy
effect: A saltwater fish; cooked it grants a temporary attack-up (Mighty).
where: Coastal salt water - Necluda Sea, Lanayru Sea, Faron coastlines.

name: Mountain Buck
effect: A male deer; hunting it yields Raw Prime Meat (a Cook-tab food).
where: Hyrule Field and Lanayru Great Spring. Skittish - use stealth and a bow.

name: Mountain Crow
effect: A black crow; shooting it down yields Raw Bird Drumstick (a Cook-tab food).
where: Highlands - Hyrule Ridge and Great Hyrule Forest.

name: Mountain Doe
effect: A female deer; hunting it yields Raw Prime Meat (a Cook-tab food).
where: Hyrule Field and Lanayru Great Spring.

name: Mountain Goat
effect: A wild goat; hunting it yields Raw Meat (a Cook-tab food).
where: Highlands - Hyrule Ridge and Great Hyrule Forest.

name: Pink Heron
effect: A pink wading bird; shooting it down yields Raw Bird Drumstick / Raw Bird Thigh (Cook-tab food).
where: Marshes and water's edge - Hyrule Ridge and Hyrule Field.

name: Razorclaw Crab
effect: A shore crab; cooked it grants a temporary attack-up (Mighty).
where: Beaches and rivers across Hyrule, common in Necluda; grab them off the shore.

name: Red-Tusked Boar
effect: A larger northern boar; hunting it yields Raw Prime Meat (a Cook-tab food).
where: Akkala Highlands and Deep Akkala forests.

name: Restless Cricket
effect: A jumpy field cricket; cooked with a monster part it makes a Hasty Elixir that boosts movement speed.
where: Hides under tall grass - Great Plateau, Hyrule Field, East Necluda. Cut grass to flush it out.

name: Rugged Rhino Beetle
effect: A tough-shelled beetle; cooked with a monster part it makes a Tough Elixir for a temporary defense boost.
where: On trees in Hyrule Field and Faron Grasslands.

name: Sand Seal
effect: A desert seal used as a mount - harpoon-and-shield-surf behind one to skim across the dunes far faster than walking. Cannot be killed for materials.
where: Wild seals roam the Gerudo Desert; sneak-grab one. Trained (red) ones rent from Gerudo Town for 20 rupees (with a shield).

name: Sand Seal (wild) / Donkey note
effect: Donkey - a domesticated pack animal kept at stables and farms. Not huntable for materials; flavor livestock (no notable drop).
where: Around stables and settlements (e.g. Highland Stable, Lurelin and other farms).

name: Sanke Carp
effect: A decorative koi-like carp; no combat effect when cooked (used in dye/quest and as a cooking base). Restores hearts.
where: Ornamental ponds and fountains - Hyrule Castle moat, Zora's Domain, town pools.

name: Seagull
effect: A coastal gull; shooting it down yields Raw Bird Drumstick (a Cook-tab food).
where: Over the Necluda Sea and Akkala Sea.

name: Sizzlefin Trout
effect: A warm-blooded trout; cooked it grants heat resistance (Spicy).
where: Warm water - Eldin Canyon streams and Goron-region pools.

name: Smotherwing Butterfly
effect: A heat-resistant butterfly; cooked with a monster part it makes a Fireproof Elixir (the main non-armor source of fire protection).
where: Scorching volcanic ground - Eldin Canyon and Death Mountain, often by lava's edge. Beedle also sells them.

name: Sneaky River Snail
effect: A river snail; cooked it boosts stealth (Sneaky). Whack it off the shore to collect.
where: Freshwater shores - West Necluda, Lanayru Great Spring, around Zora's Domain.

name: Snowcoat Fox
effect: A white northern fox; hunting it yields Raw Prime Meat or Raw Gourmet Meat (Cook-tab food).
where: Hebra Mountains and Tabantha Frontier snowfields.

name: Stalhorse
effect: An undead skeleton horse ridden by Stalkoblins. Can be mounted as a temporary mount but crumbles at sunrise (dies by ~5 AM) and cannot be registered at a stable.
where: Appears only at night - Hebra Mountains and Akkala Highlands. Defeat the Stalkoblin riding it, then hop on.

name: Staminoka Bass
effect: A long-lived bass; cooked it restores stamina (an Energizing effect).
where: Fresh water statewide; larger schools in Telta Lake, Gerudo Canyon, Squabble River.

name: Star Fragment
effect: A glowing shard of a fallen star - a rare crafting material required for the top (4-star) tier of several armor upgrades. (Not wildlife; a falling-star pickup.)
where: Falls from the night sky as a shooting star; track where it lands and grab it before it vanishes at dawn.

name: Stealthfin Trout
effect: A rare trout; cooked it boosts stealth (Sneaky).
where: Waters around the Lost Woods / Great Hyrule Forest; Octoroks there can drop them.

name: Summerwing Butterfly
effect: A warmth-loving butterfly. Cook it with a monster part for a Spicy Elixir that grants cold resistance.
where: Warm/temperate areas - Great Hyrule Forest, Eldin foothills, the Great Plateau.

name: Sunset Firefly
effect: A glowing nocturnal firefly; cooked with a monster part it makes a Sneaky Elixir that boosts stealth.
where: Near water at night - West Necluda and Great Hyrule Forest. Sneak to catch.

name: Tabantha Moose
effect: A big antlered moose; hunting it yields Raw Prime Meat or Raw Gourmet Meat - a reliable gourmet-meat source (Cook-tab food).
where: Hebra Mountains and Gerudo Highlands.

name: Thunderwing Butterfly
effect: Appears in storms; cooked with a monster part it makes an Electro Elixir for shock resistance.
where: Storm-prone areas - Hyrule Ridge, Gerudo Highlands, Faron during thunderstorms.

name: Tireless Frog
effect: A rare endurance frog; cooked with a monster part it makes an Enduring Elixir that grants bonus (yellow) stamina. Often surfaces in the rain.
where: Near water - Lanayru Great Spring and Hyrule Ridge. Uncommon.

name: Voltfin Trout
effect: An electric trout; cooked it grants shock resistance (Electro).
where: Hyrule Ridge and Tabantha Frontier waters; also dropped by Forest Octoroks near Thundra Plateau.

name: Warm Darner
effect: A dragonfly that thrives in cold; cooked with a monster part it makes a Spicy Elixir for cold resistance.
where: Warmer zones - Akkala Highlands and Hyrule Field. Also sold by Beedle.

name: Wasteland Coyote
effect: A desert canine; killing it yields Raw Prime Meat (a Cook-tab food).
where: Gerudo Desert and Gerudo Highlands.

name: Water Buffalo
effect: A large wetland bovine; hunting it yields Raw Prime Meat, or Raw Gourmet Meat from the biggest ones (Cook-tab food).
where: Lanayru Wetlands and Akkala Highlands.

name: White Goat
effect: A white wild goat; hunting it yields Raw Meat (a Cook-tab food).
where: West Necluda and Eldin Canyon.

name: White Horse
effect: A pure-white royal horse descended from Princess Zelda's mount - high stats and the Royal Saddle & Bridle reward after registering it.
where: Roams Safula Hill (Sanidin Park Ruins) in Hyrule Ridge, past Manhala Bridge north of Outskirt Stable. Tied to the 'Royal White Stallion' side quest.

name: Winterwing Butterfly
effect: A cold-loving butterfly with frost-tipped wings. Cooked with a monster part it brews a Chilly Elixir for heat resistance.
where: Cold areas - Hyrule Ridge, Tabantha Frontier, Hebra. Sneak up and grab it.

name: Wood Pigeon
effect: A plump forest pigeon; shooting it down yields Raw Bird Drumstick / Raw Whole Bird (Cook-tab food). Rare variants (Rainbow/White/Hotfeather Pigeon) are the same compendium creature.
where: Wooded areas - Great Hyrule Forest and Hyrule Ridge.

name: Woodland Boar
effect: A common wild boar; hunting it yields Raw Meat (a Cook-tab food).
where: Forests and fields - Hyrule Field and Lanayru Great Spring. Sneak-shoot or it bolts.
